name: West & South Slavic Challenge
description: Distinguish between Czech, Polish, Slovak, Slovene, Croatian, and Serbian (Latinica).

questions.text: Mluvím trochu česky, ale stále se učím.
questions.explanation: The 'ů' and 'v' at the start of 'všichni' or 'vlak' are common; 'česky' without an 'i' at the end is typical for the adverb.

questions.text: Děkuji vám za vaši pomoc a laskavost.
questions.explanation: The 'ě' character is a primary marker for Czech text.

questions.text: Přeji vám hezký den a hodně štěstí.
questions.explanation: The 'ř' (přeji) and 'šť' cluster are unmistakable Czech features.

questions.text: Chtěl bych si objednat šálek kávy.
questions.explanation: The combination of 'chtěl' (l-participle) and 'šálek' is specific to Czech vocabulary.

questions.text: Kde najdu nejbližší stanici metra?
questions.explanation: The superlative prefix 'nej-' followed by the root is common across West Slavic, but 'nejbližší' with 'žš' is very Czech.

questions.text: Tento dům je velmi starý a krásný.
questions.explanation: Czech uses 'velmi' for 'very' and 'krásný' with the long 'á'.

questions.text: Mohl byste mi prosím ukázat cestu?
questions.explanation: The conditional 'byste' and the word 'cestu' (accusative of cesta) appear here.

questions.text: Dnes je venku opravdu horké počasí.
questions.explanation: The word 'počasí' (weather) ends in 'í', whereas Slovak uses 'počasie'.

questions.text: Musíme jít do obchodu koupit chleba.
questions.explanation: The verb 'jít' (to go) and 'koupit' (to buy) are standard Czech infinitives.

questions.text: V kolik hodin začíná ten koncert?
questions.explanation: The phrase 'V kolik hodin' is the standard way to ask 'at what time'.

questions.text: Mám rád čtení knih v češtině.
questions.explanation: Verbal nouns ending in '-ní' and the language name 'češtině' identify this.

questions.text: Věříte, že se situace brzy zlepší?
questions.explanation: Uses 'ě' and the future tense construction 'se zlepší'.

questions.text: Můj bratr pracuje jako programátor v Praze.
questions.explanation: The locative case 'v Praze' (in Prague) shows the typical consonant shift.

questions.text: Užijte si víkend se svou rodinou.
questions.explanation: The imperative 'užijte' and 'rodinou' (instrumental case) are standard.

questions.text: Pamatujete si na našeho starého učitele?
questions.explanation: The 'ou' ending in 'našeho' and 'učitele' are Czech grammatical markers.

questions.text: To je skvělý nápad, pojďme to udělat.
questions.explanation: The word 'nápad' (idea) and 'udělat' (to do/make) are key.

questions.text: Zítra bude pršet, nezapomeňte si deštník.
questions.explanation: The future 'bude' and the specific word 'deštník' (umbrella).

questions.text: Cesta trvala déle, než jsme čekali.
questions.explanation: The comparative 'déle' and the 'jsme' auxiliary for past tense.

questions.text: Můžete mi půjčit tu tužku?
questions.explanation: The verb 'půjčit' and the word 'tužka' (pencil) are typical.

questions.text: Dívám se na televizi každý večer.
questions.explanation: The verb 'dívám se' (I watch) and 'každý' (every) are Czech.

questions.text: Bardzo dziękuję za Twoją pomoc.
questions.explanation: The nasal 'ę' in 'dziękuję' is a definitive Polish marker.

questions.text: Czy mówisz po angielsku lub po polsku?
questions.explanation: The question marker 'Czy' is unique to Polish among these six.

questions.text: Chciałbym zamówić kolację dla dwóch osób.
questions.explanation: The 'ł' character (l with a stroke) and the 'sz'/'cz' sounds are very Polish.

questions.text: Gdzie znajduje się najbliższa apteka?
questions.explanation: The cluster 'najbliższa' with 'ż' and 'sz' is Polish orthography.

questions.text: Dzisiaj jest bardzo ładna pogoda.
questions.explanation: The word 'dzisiaj' (today) and 'bardzo' (very) are Polish.

questions.text: Przepraszam, nie zrozumiałem, co powiedziałeś.
questions.explanation: The 'prz' digraph and the nasal 'ą' in 'zrozumiałem' identify this.

questions.text: Moja siostra mieszka w Warszawie od lat.
questions.explanation: The spelling of 'Warszawie' (Warsaw) and the 'sz' digraph.

questions.text: Lubię jeść pierogi z kapustą i grzybami.
questions.explanation: The nasal 'ą' in 'kapustą' and the 'rz' in 'grzybami' are Polish.

questions.text: Jutro rano muszę iść do pracy.
questions.explanation: The word 'jutro' (tomorrow) is shared, but the overall sentence structure is Polish.

questions.text: Proszę, usiądź i poczekaj chwilę.
questions.explanation: The 'sz' and 'ś' in 'proszę' and 'usiądź' are typical Polish sounds.

questions.text: Cieszę się, że cię widzę.
questions.explanation: The 'sz' and nasal 'ę' in 'cieszę' are Polish identifiers.

questions.text: To jest mój ulubiony samochód.
questions.explanation: The 'ó' (o with acute) is used in Polish (and Czech), but 'samochód' is the Polish word for car.

questions.text: Nigdy nie widziałem tak pięknego miasta.
questions.explanation: The 'ł' and 'ę' in 'widziałem' and 'pięknego' are Polish.

questions.text: Czy możesz mi pożyczyć trochę pieniędzy?
questions.explanation: The 'ż' and nasal 'ę' in 'pieniędzy' are distinctively Polish.

questions.text: Wszystko będzie dobrze, nie martw się.
questions.explanation: The 'sz' in 'wszystko' and the 'ę' in 'będzie' mark this as Polish.

questions.text: Mój ojciec jest inżynierem.
questions.explanation: The 'ż' and the nasal 'em' ending for instrumental case.

questions.text: Polska to kraj o bogatej historii.
questions.explanation: The word 'kraj' is common, but the 'ej' ending in 'bogatej' is Polish.

questions.text: Idziemy do kina w przyszły piątek.
questions.explanation: The 'sz' and 'y' in 'przyszły' and the nasal 'ą' in 'piątek'.

questions.text: Zawsze piję herbatę rano.
questions.explanation: The word 'herbata' (tea) is unique to Polish (compared to čaj in others) and the nasal 'ę' in 'piję'.

questions.text: Nie mam pojęcia, jak to zrobić.
questions.explanation: The 'ę' in 'pojęcia' and the word 'zrobić' are Polish.

questions.text: Hovorím trochu po slovensky.
questions.explanation: Slovak uses 'vsky' or 'cky' for adverbs, and 'hovorím' is 'to speak'.

questions.text: Veľmi pekne vám ďakujem za pomoc.
questions.explanation: The 'ľ' (l with caron) and 'ď' are common in Slovak orthography.

questions.text: Prajeme vám veľa úspechov v práci.
questions.explanation: The 'ľ' in 'veľa' and 'ú' in 'úspechov' are Slovak indicators.

questions.text: Kde sa nachádza najbližšia zastávka?
questions.explanation: The 'ia' in 'nachádza' and the word 'zastávka' distinguish it from Czech 'stanice'.

questions.text: Môžem vás poprosiť o pohár vody?
questions.explanation: The 'ô' (o with circumflex) is unique to Slovak among these languages.

questions.text: Dnes je vonku skutočne horúco.
questions.explanation: The 'ú' in 'horúco' and the word 'vonku' (outside) are Slovak.

questions.text: Môj brat pracuje ako lekár v Bratislave.
questions.explanation: The 'ô' in 'Môj' is the key identifier here.

questions.text: Ideme na výlet do Vysokých Tatier.
questions.explanation: The plural 'Tatier' and the specific vowel 'ý' in 'Vysokých'.

questions.text: Prosím, posaďte sa a chvíľu počkajte.
questions.explanation: The 'ľ' in 'chvíľu' is a classic Slovak marker.

questions.text: Slovensko je krásna krajina s horami.
questions.explanation: The word 'Slovensko' and 'krajina' (country) identify the language.

questions.text: Všetko bude v poriadku, neboj sa.
questions.explanation: The cluster 'ia' in 'poriadku' is more common in Slovak than Czech 'o'.

questions.text: Rád čítam knihy v slovenčine.
questions.explanation: The language name 'slovenčine' and the lack of 'ř' helps differentiate from Czech.

questions.text: Dúfam, že sa čoskoro uvidíme.
questions.explanation: The 'ú' in 'Dúfam' and the word 'čoskoro' (soon) are Slovak.

questions.text: Máte nejaké otázky k téme?
questions.explanation: The 'ia' in 'otázky' and the word 'nejaké' are standard Slovak.

questions.text: Bývam v malom byte na okraji mesta.
questions.explanation: The verb 'bývam' (I live/reside) is common to West Slavic, but 'byte' (apartment) fits Slovak.

questions.text: Tento týždeň mám veľa práce.
questions.explanation: The 'ý' and 'ž' in 'týždeň' (week) and 'ľ' in 'veľa'.

questions.text: Chcem si kúpiť nový mobilný telefón.
questions.explanation: The 'ú' in 'kúpiť' distinguishes it from Czech 'koupit'.

questions.text: Cesta bola veľmi dlhá a únavná.
questions.explanation: The 'ľ' in 'veľmi' and the 'ú' in 'únavná'.

questions.text: Počasie sa má zajtra zlepšiť.
questions.explanation: The word 'počasie' (weather) with '-ie' is Slovak.

questions.text: Ďakujem za tvoju trpezlivosť.
questions.explanation: The 'Ď' and the 'v' instead of 'u' in 'trpezlivosť'.

questions.text: Ali govorite slovensko?
questions.explanation: The question marker 'Ali' and the 'o' ending for the language name.

questions.text: Najlepša hvala za vašo pomoč.
questions.explanation: The 'š' and the ending 'o' in 'vašo' are typical Slovene.

questions.text: Kje se nahaja najbližja avtobusna postaja?
questions.explanation: The word 'Kje' (where) and 'postaja' (station) are Slovene.

questions.text: Danes je zelo lep in sončen dan.
questions.explanation: The word 'zelo' (very) and 'danes' (today) are Slovene.

questions.text: Želim si naročiti skodelico kave.
questions.explanation: The word 'naročiti' (to order) and 'skodelico' (cup) are Slovene.

questions.text: Moja sestra živi v Ljubljani.
questions.explanation: The locative 'v Ljubljani' uses the standard Slovene ending.

questions.text: Rad berem knjige v slovenščini.
questions.explanation: The 'šč' in 'slovenščini' is a common Slovene cluster.

questions.text: Vsi ljudje so enakovredni.
questions.explanation: The word 'Vsi' (all) is a classic Slovene identifier.

questions.text: Ali mi lahko pokažete pot na zemljevidu?
questions.explanation: The word 'zemljevidu' (map) and the question word 'Ali'.

questions.text: Jutri bo deževalo, ne pozabi dežnika.
questions.explanation: The verb 'deževalo' (it will rain) comes from 'dež' (rain).

questions.text: Vesel sem, da sva se spoznala.
questions.explanation: The use of the dual form 'sva' (we two) is unique to Slovene among these.

questions.text: To je moja najljubša barva.
questions.explanation: The word 'barva' (color) is used in Slovene and Croatian, but 'najljubša' is the Slovene form.

questions.text: Moramo iti v trgovino po kruh.
questions.explanation: The 'iti' infinitive and the word 'trgovino' (shop).

questions.text: Kateri dan je danes?
questions.explanation: The question word 'Kateri' (which) is Slovene.

questions.text: Slovenija ima čudovito naravo.
questions.explanation: The adjective 'čudovito' (wonderful) is very common in Slovene.

questions.text: Ali veš, kje je ključ?
questions.explanation: The question starter 'Ali' and the word 'ključ' (key).

questions.text: Zvečer rad gledam televizijo.
questions.explanation: The adverb 'Zvečer' (in the evening) and 'gledam' (I watch).

questions.text: Pridi sem in mi pomagaj.
questions.explanation: The imperative 'Pridi' (come) and 'pomagaj' (help).

questions.text: Njegov avto je zelo hiter.
questions.explanation: The word 'hiter' (fast) and 'zelo' (very).

questions.text: Lepo se imejte na počitnicah.
questions.explanation: The phrase 'Lepo se imejte' (have a nice time) is typical.

questions.text: Govorite li hrvatski? Ja još učim.
questions.explanation: The question particle 'li' and the word 'hrvatski' (Croatian).

questions.text: Puno vam hvala na vašoj pomoći.
questions.explanation: Croatian uses 'puno' (a lot) and 'hvala' commonly.

questions.text: Gdje se nalazi najbliža ljekarna?
questions.explanation: The word 'ljekarna' (pharmacy) is a specifically Croatian word, distinct from Serbian 'apoteka'.

questions.text: Danas je vrijeme veoma lijepo i sunčano.
questions.explanation: The Ijekavian 'vrijeme' and 'lijepo' are standard in Croatian.

questions.text: Želio bih naručiti šalicu kave.
questions.explanation: The Ijekavian 'želio' and the word 'šalicu' (cup).

questions.text: Moja sestra živi u Zagrebu već godinama.
questions.explanation: The locative 'u Zagrebu' and the word 'već' (already).

questions.text: Volim čitati knjige na hrvatskom jeziku.
questions.explanation: The preposition 'na' followed by the locative 'jeziku' is the standard way to say 'in a language'.

questions.text: Svi ljudi su slobodni i jednaki.
questions.explanation: While similar to others, the combination is typical of the South Slavic group.

questions.text: Možete li mi pokazati put na karti?
questions.explanation: The word 'karta' (map) is used in Croatian, whereas Serbian might use 'mapa'.

questions.text: Sutra će padati kiša, ne zaboravite kišobran.
questions.explanation: The future tense with 'će' and the word 'kišobran' (umbrella).

questions.text: Drago mi je što smo se upoznali.
questions.explanation: The conjunction 'što' is frequently used in Croatian instead of Serbian 'da'.

questions.text: To je moja omiljena boja.
questions.explanation: The word 'omiljena' (favorite) and 'boja' (color).

questions.text: Moramo ići u trgovinu po kruh.
questions.explanation: Croatian uses 'kruh' for bread (Serbian often uses 'hleb').

questions.text: Koji je danas datum?
questions.explanation: The question word 'Koji' (which) and 'datum'.

questions.text: Hrvatska ima prekrasnu obalu i more.
questions.explanation: The word 'prekrasnu' (beautiful) and 'obalu' (coast).

questions.text: Znate li gdje se nalazi kolodvor?
questions.explanation: The word 'kolodvor' (station) is uniquely Croatian compared to Serbian 'stanica'.

questions.text: Navečer volim gledati televiziju.
questions.explanation: The adverb 'Navečer' (in the evening) is typical.

questions.text: Dođi ovamo i pomozi mi.
questions.explanation: The imperative 'Dođi' and 'pomozi'.

questions.text: Njegov auto je vrlo brz.
questions.explanation: The word 'vrlo' (very) and 'brz' (fast).

questions.text: Lijepo se provedite na odmoru.
questions.explanation: The Ijekavian 'Lijepo' and 'odmoru' (vacation).

questions.text: Govorite li srpski? Ja još uvek učim.
questions.explanation: The phrase 'još uvek' (still) is more common in Serbian than Croatian 'još'.

questions.text: Mnogo vam hvala na vašoj pomoći.
questions.explanation: Serbian frequently uses 'mnogo' (much/a lot) where Croatian uses 'puno'.

questions.text: Gde se nalazi najbliža apoteka?
questions.explanation: The Ekavian 'Gde' and the word 'apoteka' (pharmacy) are standard Serbian.

questions.text: Danas je vreme veoma lepo i sunčano.
questions.explanation: The Ekavian 'vreme' and 'lepo' identify this as Serbian (Latin script).

questions.text: Želeo bih da naručim šolju kafe.
questions.explanation: The Ekavian 'želeo' and the word 'šolju' (cup) are Serbian markers.

questions.text: Moja sestra živi u Beogradu godinama.
questions.explanation: The city 'Beograd' and the locative 'u Beogradu'.

questions.text: Volim da čitam knjige na srpskom jeziku.
questions.explanation: Serbian prefers the 'da + present tense' construction over the infinitive.

questions.text: Sva ljudska bića se rađaju slobodna.
questions.explanation: While identical to Croatian, the context of other clues usually distinguishes them.

questions.text: Možete li da mi pokažete put na mapi?
questions.explanation: The use of 'da mi pokažete' (that you show me) and 'mapa'.

questions.text: Sutra će padati kiša, ne zaboravite kišobran.
questions.explanation: Serbian uses 'kišobran' like Croatian, but usually in Ekavian context.

questions.text: Drago mi je da smo se upoznali.
questions.explanation: Serbian prefers 'da' as a conjunction where Croatian often uses 'što'.

questions.text: To je moja omiljena boja.
questions.explanation: Identical to Croatian, but standard in Serbian Latinica as well.

questions.text: Moramo da idemo u prodavnicu po hleb.
questions.explanation: The word 'prodavnicu' (shop) and 'hleb' (bread) are Serbian identifiers.

questions.text: Koji je danas datum?
questions.explanation: Standard South Slavic, but part of the Serbian set.

questions.text: Srbija ima prelepu prirodu i planine.
questions.explanation: The word 'Srbija' and 'prelepu' (beautiful - Ekavian).

questions.text: Znate li gde se nalazi stanica?
questions.explanation: The Ekavian 'gde' and the word 'stanica' (station) differentiate it from Croatian.

questions.text: Uveče volim da gledam televiziju.
questions.explanation: The adverb 'Uveče' and the 'da + present' construction.

questions.text: Dođi ovamo i pomozi mi.
questions.explanation: Standard South Slavic imperative.

questions.text: Njegov auto je veoma brz.
questions.explanation: Serbian uses 'veoma' or 'mnogo' more frequently than 'vrlo'.

questions.text: Lepo se provedite na odmoru.
questions.explanation: The Ekavian 'Lepo' is the key marker here.
